summary: Three phases: (1) seam inventory + anchor-case ruling surface, (2) per-seam atomic removals (1 commit per seam, ruling-blocked seams skipped until ruled), (3) closeout-ready handoff. Frozen-fixture invariant + AC-05a/b real-world identity are non-negotiable gates per commit.

phases.label: Seam inventory + anchor-case ruling surface

tasks.subject: Walk src/integration/rag/* and produce seam inventory
operations: Grep src/integration/rag/* for: `try {` `} catch`, `?? null`, `?? {}`, `|| {}`, `|| []`, `as any`, `Number(`, `fileExists`, `existsSync`, async catch blocks, optional-chaining-with-fallback patterns
Eyeball-read every .ts file in src/integration/rag/* and src/integration/rag/__tests__/*
Classify each candidate: silent_skip | swallowed_error | dead_branch | pass_through | type_coercion_fallback | justified_keep
For each candidate, propose disposition: delete | promote_to_explicit_failure | keep_with_justification
If disposition is keep_with_justification, the inventory entry MUST name the failure mode (criterion-3 from stub)

tasks.subject: Author inventory.md in WO directory
operations: Create coderef/workorder/rag-index-seam-deletion/inventory.md
Document grep methodology so future audits can re-run it
Table per file: seam | lines | classification | disposition | rationale
Anchor case (chunk-converter.ts:60-63) is enumerated with all three options (A/B/C) verbatim from context.json scope_decision_required block — flag as RULING_REQUIRED
Surface any other ambiguous seam (multiple legitimate paths) as RULING_REQUIRED for ORCHESTRATOR

tasks.subject: Phase 1 commit + handoff for anchor-case ruling
operations: Commit message: 'inventory(WO-RAG-INDEX-SEAM-DELETION-001): seam audit of src/integration/rag/* + anchor-case ruling required'
tsc --noEmit clean both configs (no production code changes in Phase 1, but verify nothing inadvertently touched)
Update communication.json with Phase 1 complete + ruling-required block citing inventory.md
HALT: do NOT begin Phase 2 atomic removals until ORCHESTRATOR rules on anchor case (and any other RULING_REQUIRED seams)

phases.commit_message_template: inventory(WO-RAG-INDEX-SEAM-DELETION-001): seam audit + anchor-case A/B/C ruling required
phases.phase_gate: Phase 1 commit landed AND inventory.md authored AND tsc clean AND ruling-required block surfaced in communication.json. ORCHESTRATOR rules on anchor case (+ any other surfaced RULING_REQUIRED seams) before Phase 2 begins.

phases.label: Per-seam atomic removals (post-ruling)

tasks.subject: For each seam in inventory.md with disposition=delete or disposition=promote, author one atomic commit
operations: Edit src/integration/rag/<file>.ts to remove or promote the seam
Update tests in src/integration/rag/__tests__/<file>.test.ts that exercised the silent branch (silent-skip → explicit-failure assertion, or delete if branch was dead)
tsc --noEmit clean both configs
vitest run src/integration/rag/__tests__/ — frozen-fixture invariant + all rag-suite tests PASS
vitest run __tests__/pipeline/ — Phase 7 chokepoint INVARIANT + AC-05 dual-identity PASS
Optional: rerun verify-dual-ac.mjs against real codebase; AC-05a 2415===2415 + AC-05b 263===263 still PASS
Commit message: per-seam template noting file:lines, disposition, rationale, gate results

tasks.subject: Halt-and-report triggers
operations: HALT if frozen-fixture invariant trips on the post-removal state
HALT if AC-05a/b real-world identity shifts on the post-removal state (real codebase static probe)
HALT if a seam initially classified delete or promote turns out to handle a real failure mode (reclassify as keep_with_justification, document in inventory.md update commit, do NOT remove)
HALT if any change required outside src/integration/rag/* + src/integration/rag/__tests__/* (boundary violation)
HALT if any new SkipReason enum value or IndexingResult schema field is needed beyond what ORCHESTRATOR ruled in the anchor-case ruling (DR-PHASE-7-B)

phases.phase_gate: All inventory.md disposition=delete and disposition=promote seams have landed as atomic commits AND every commit passed tsc + frozen-fixture invariant + AC-05 dual-identity. Real-world substrate identity (AC-05a 2415, AC-05b 263) preserved. LOC delta net negative.

phases.label: Closeout-ready handoff

tasks.subject: Final accounting + closeout marker
operations: Update inventory.md with final per-seam outcomes (delete | promote | keep_with_justification + commit SHAs)
Update communication.json: status=complete, completed_at, current_phase=2, phase_status=closeout_ready
Append CODEREF-CORE → ORCHESTRATOR communication_log entry: commit chain summary, total LOC delta, per-seam outcome table, frozen-fixture invariant + AC-05 + real-world identity preservation evidence
Hand off to ORCHESTRATOR for cross-project /close-workorder dispatch

phases.phase_gate: Communication.json closeout-ready. ORCHESTRATOR dispatches SKILLS for cross-project /close-workorder.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Phase 1 inventory.md exists in WO directory with grep methodology + per-file per-seam classification + disposition + rationale
Anchor case (chunk-converter.ts:60-63) resolved per ORCHESTRATOR ruling on Option A/B/C; resolution applied in a Phase 2 atomic commit
Each Phase 2 atomic commit removes or promotes exactly one seam (no bundled removals)
Each Phase 2 commit passes tsc --noEmit clean both configs
acceptance_criteria.verification: Per-commit tsc evidence in commit message or close report

acceptance_criteria.statement: Frozen-fixture CI-blocking invariant PASSES at every Phase 2 commit
acceptance_criteria.verification: vitest run on src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/dual-ac-frozen/ test path per commit

acceptance_criteria.statement: AC-05a real-world 2415===2415 + AC-05b real-world 263===263 PASS at WO close (verified via predecessor WO static probe verify-dual-ac.mjs)
acceptance_criteria.verification: static probe output captured in close report

acceptance_criteria.statement: Phase 7 chokepoint INVARIANT block (lines 32-70 of __tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts) untouched
acceptance_criteria.verification: git diff over WO range shows zero changes to lines 32-70

acceptance_criteria.statement: All Phase 2 changes are within src/integration/rag/* + src/integration/rag/__tests__/* boundary
acceptance_criteria.verification: git diff over WO range shows no changes outside this radius
git diff shows zero changes to that file
git diff shows zero changes to the fixture directory

acceptance_criteria.statement: No new SkipReason enum value or IndexingResult schema field added beyond what ORCHESTRATOR rules on the anchor case (DR-PHASE-7-B)
acceptance_criteria.verification: git diff inspection of types.ts and IndexingResult definitions

acceptance_criteria.statement: Net negative LOC across the WO commit range (real cleanup, not zero-sum)
acceptance_criteria.verification: git diff --stat over fcea78a..(close) shows deletions exceed insertions

acceptance_criteria.statement: Each disposition=keep_with_justification seam carries a single-sentence comment naming the failure mode it handles (per stub criterion #3)
acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspection of post-WO source for inventory keeps

acceptance_criteria.statement: Predecessor archives untouched (rag-index-frozen-fixture-dual-ac-invariants, rag-index-single-analyzer-slice, cleanup-indexing-orchestrator-analyzerservice-param, path-normalization-fix, pipeline-indexing-rag)
acceptance_criteria.verification: git diff over WO range shows zero changes under coderef/archived/

acceptance_criteria.statement: Cultural validator scope-mode 11/11 PASS + tracking validator scope-mode 15/15 PASS at close
acceptance_criteria.verification: Both validators pass at close time

acceptance_criteria.statement: /close-workorder produces ARCHIVED.md + tracking ledger updates + workorder_completed/closed events; STUB-RAG-INDEX-SEAM-DELETION-001 closed_via_workorder

decisions_record.rationale: The seam list is unknown at scoping time; that's the work of Phase 1. Skipping Phase 1 (e.g., scoping individual removals upfront) would require SKILLS or ORCHESTRATOR to do the inventory at scoping time, which puts the load-bearing decision in the wrong phase. Phased structure also accommodates multiple ruling-required seams (anchor case + any others surfaced in Phase 1).

decisions_record.decision: Anchor case (chunk-converter.ts:60-63) ruling deferred to ORCHESTRATOR via scope_decision_required block; SKILLS does not pre-rule
decisions_record.rationale: All three options (A/B/C) have legitimate trade-offs documented in the stub. The choice depends on (a) whether DR-PHASE-7-B is hard or negotiable, (b) whether phantom imports actually occur on real coderef-core scans (data Phase 1 may surface), (c) the degree to which 'earn keep via observable mechanism' is treated as non-negotiable for bulletproof #3. Per dispatch-047 directive: 'If anchor case ambiguous, surface for ORCHESTRATOR ruling.' SKILLS judges it ambiguous and surfaces. Ruling-required block lives in context.scope.scope_decision_required.

decisions_record.decision: Per-commit verification gates include both frozen-fixture CI invariant AND optional real-codebase static probe (verify-dual-ac.mjs from predecessor WO archive)
decisions_record.rationale: The frozen fixture has no phantom-import case. If a Phase 2 commit removes a seam that affects phantom-import behavior, the synthetic-input invariant won't catch a real-codebase regression. The static probe rerun is the secondary guard. Marked optional because it requires a real coderef-core scan (~25min if live rag-index, but verify-dual-ac.mjs is static so faster); execution can decide cadence — at minimum at Phase 3 closeout.

decisions_record.decision: Frozen-fixture file contents are EXEMPT from this WO's edits; if a seam removal requires fixture changes, HALT and reframe rather than amend the fixture
decisions_record.rationale: The fixture is the safety net. Modifying it during a seam-deletion WO would defeat the safety-net property — the test could be silently weakened to accommodate a problematic removal. If a seam genuinely cannot be removed without fixture change, that's a signal the seam earns keep OR that the fixture itself has a gap (which would be a separate WO).

decisions_record.decision: Disposition=keep_with_justification is a legitimate Phase 2 outcome; not every inventoried seam must be removed
decisions_record.rationale: Earning-keep criteria from stub specify when a seam earns keep. Phase 1 inventory may classify some candidates as keep_with_justification. The WO target is 'remove seams that don't earn keep,' not 'remove every seam.' False-positive prevention: per-seam disposition gives the WO author license to leave a seam in place if it handles a real failure mode, with a single-sentence comment naming the failure mode.

estimated_loc_delta: -25 to -75 net (5-15 seams × ~-5 LOC average; Phase 1 finalizes the count)
